I finally was able to mitigate the consistant freezing that the gpu was causing, and after 24 hours of continuous suystem running under various loads, with local ai models running, i am convinced that the issue is officially fixed. Scaffold the automated scheduling and publishing layers for the "Instaminion" framework profile engine by creating three tightly coupled modules:

1. Create `core/ingest.go`: 
   - Define a `StateTracker` struct that reads and writes to a local `state.json` tracker file.
   - Implement a directory watcher that scans a target content folder for media files (.jpg, .png, .mp4).
   - Track state parameters: which assets are pending, which have been successfully deployed, and timestamps of the last execution block to maintain absolute stateless tracking for the underlying worker loops.

2. Create `integrations/instagram.go`:
   - Implement an `InstagramClient` struct that reads an encrypted long-lived user access token, an Instagram Business Account ID, and utilizes a standard net/http client wrapper.
   - Implement the Meta Graph API Content Publishing pipeline precisely using a clean three-step execution dance:
     a) Step 1 (Container Creation): Issue a POST request to `https://graph.facebook.com/v22.0/{ig-user-id}/media` to submit the file payload and caption copy, returning a `container_id`.
     b) Step 2 (Status Polling): Implement a loop using a time.Ticker that calls `GET /{container_id}?fields=status_code` every 5 seconds, blocking until the status returns as "FINISHED".
     c) Step 3 (Final Publish): Issue a POST request to `https://graph.facebook.com/v22.0/{ig-user-id}/media_publish` passing the `creation_id` to make the post live on the targeted profile.

3. Create `core/scheduler.go`:
   - Implement a time-based background loop using a native `time.Ticker` set to a user-defined interval.
   - When the ticker signals an execution block: parse the roadmap, extract an unposted asset via `ingest.go`, submit a payload to our `instagram_creator` profile pool to generate captions/hashtags from Gemma, and route the final generated parameters straight to the `InstagramClient` publishing module.
